name: Accomack-Northampton Planning District Commisssion
description_short: Regional planning and community development commission providing housing services and planning assistance to Accomack County, Northampton County, and Eastern Shore Virginia municipalities.
description_long: The Accomack-Northampton Planning District Commission (A-NPDC) is a government planning agency serving the Eastern Shore of Virginia, including Accomack County, Northampton County, the Town of Chincoteague, and incorporated towns throughout the region. Established as a public sector organization, the A-NPDC operates as a planning and community development authority rather than a commercial financial services provider. The organization provides planning services, community development support, and housing-related assistance to its service area. The A-NPDC operates three distinct boards: the main Planning District Commission (meets monthly on third Mondays), the Accomack-Northampton Regional Housing Authority (meets bimonthly), and the Eastern Shore of Virginia Housing Alliance (meets bimonthly). All meetings are open to the public and held at the Enterprise Building. What distinguishes this organization is its non-profit, government-backed approach to regional development and housing issues. Rather than providing direct consumer financial products, the A-NPDC focuses on strategic planning, policy development, and housing advocacy. The organization completed a comprehensive Regional Plan 2028 following community input from over 580 surveys collected in 2015. The A-NPDC is committed to Title VI Civil Rights compliance and serves diverse populations through multilingual website support in English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole. For consumers seeking direct financial assistance or credit repair services, the A-NPDC is not the appropriate resource. This is a planning and policy organization best suited for understanding regional housing initiatives, attending public meetings about community development, or seeking information about housing programs affiliated with the Regional Housing Authority.
pros: Provides housing services and community development support specific to Eastern Shore Virginia region
Multiple affiliated organizations (NRHA and ESVHA) dedicated to regional housing initiatives
All board meetings are open to the public for community participation
Offers multilingual website access in English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole
Completed comprehensive regional strategic planning based on 582 community surveys
Commitment to Title VI Civil Rights compliance and non-discrimination
Free planning and community development resources at no cost to residents
cons: Not a consumer financial services provider—does not offer credit counseling, loans, or credit repair
Limited direct consumer assistance; primarily focuses on regional policy and planning rather than individual financial help
Website does not clearly describe specific housing programs or services available to individual consumers
No indication of HUD-approved housing counselors or certified credit counseling staff on website
Serves only specific geographic region (Accomack County, Northampton County, and select Eastern Shore municipalities)
best_for: Eastern Shore Virginia residents seeking regional housing initiative information
Communities and municipalities looking for planning and development assistance
Individuals interested in public participation in regional housing policy decisions
Non-profit organizations and governments collaborating on regional development projects
diagnosis: The Accomack-Northampton Planning District Commission is a government planning agency, not a consumer financial services provider, and is best suited for residents seeking information about regional housing initiatives and community development rather than direct personal financial assistance. Consumers needing credit counseling, debt help, or financial services should seek other resources.
services: Regional strategic planning and policy development
Housing services and housing authority administration
Regional housing alliance operations and coordination
Public meetings and board governance (monthly and bimonthly)
Title VI Civil Rights compliance and oversight
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
